data.title: As the doc-writer agent, I write dev-team documentation to the correct project-local directories
data.description:
Scope: Update plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md to:
1. Add docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/ to the 4-tier schema using positive enumeration framing
2. Add a NEVER-statement prohibiting writing to plugins/dso/
3. Move the plugin boundary constraint to the Role/Constraints section (top of file, before any procedure)

The constraint placement at the top of the file is required to guard against positional bias — agents in long-context sessions weight constraints encountered early more heavily than those encountered in later procedure sections.

The 4-tier schema update: Add these directories as a new entry (e.g., under Tier 2 user-facing or as a separate dev-team artifacts tier). The schema must positively enumerate: docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/ with descriptions of what belongs in each.

Terminology must match the CLAUDE.md plugin boundary rule (S3) exactly — same directory names, same NEVER phrasing.

Done Definitions:
- [ ] plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md Role/Constraints section (at or near top of file) contains: list of permitted project-local artifact directories (docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/) AND NEVER write to plugins/dso/
- [ ] Plugin boundary constraint appears before Section 1 (The Bright Line Decision Engine) in the file
- [ ] The 4-tier schema section names docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/ as valid output locations with descriptions
- [ ] Terminology matches S3 CLAUDE.md rule exactly — run diff check before closing
- [ ] A behavioral test in tests/ verifies the routing rule: given a design artifact request, the agent prompt routes to docs/designs/ not plugins/dso/docs/designs/

Considerations:
- [Testing] Behavioral test must check routing decision (destination path), not file content — assert the path the agent is directed to write to, not what it writes
- [Testing] Use the behavioral testing standard (plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/behavioral-testing-standard.md) — test observable behavior, not implementation details
- [Maintainability] Coordinate terminology with S3 before closing — both stories touch the same conceptual constraint in different files

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption could send implementation in the wrong direction; document all assumptions made without escalating.
